initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of complete right bundle branch block?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose complete right bundle branch block, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
Reduction in QRS amplitude in limb leads
Variable QRS duration across different beats

finding_identification.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the QRS duration prolonged on this ECG? Note that a QRS duration is considered to be prolonged if it is greater than 120 milliseconds.

ecg_grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe a QRS wave that demonstrates a non-prolonged QRS duration? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into?

diagnostic_decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this ECG suggest the presence of complete right bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
